rationale.R1.2: A generic request to check the excerpt centrally includes verifying whether the argument from reported results to conclusions is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes explicit performance and generalization claims that should be checked against the reported benchmark results.
rationale.R1.6: A broad check of a paper excerpt reasonably includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague checking request must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and type of review.
rationale.RX.2: The excerpt contains strong claims such as state-of-the-art advancement and superiority, making overclaim control active.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is only to check the provided artifact, so staying within the excerpt and not executing broader review work is required.
rationale.RX.4: Claims about model improvements and drop-in replacement could mislead downstream readers if unchecked, so harm avoidance is active.
rationale.RX.5: Assessment should remain traceable to the provided text and its reported figures rather than unsupported external assumptions.
